title: Stats & Growth
subtitle: Five stats, one bonus table, the training funnel

content.text: Asset development schedule, contractor class. Training appreciates the asset. Augmentation amortizes it.
Five stats. One bonus table. No levels, no XP — your credits are your experience points.
The Five Stats
Every roll adds exactly one stat bonus; the weapon or skill names which stat it uses. The five, and where each one shows up:

rows: The frame — raw physical force. Powers grapples alongside Athletics; meets the thresholds that one-hand heavy weapons (Dual Wield & Titan Grip).
Speed. Buys initiative — going first, claiming activation slots, winning ties (Turn, AP & Reactions) — and feeds Evasion (Attacks & the Shell Clock).
The dying-well stat. The Fate Roll leans on Grit (The Staircase), and venting Charge at encounter's end sheds up to your Grit bonus (Charge & Saturation).
The link to pattern-work. Control and improvisation Checks when casting roll Rift Lore dice + Interface (Weave & Fire).
Feeds Evasion alongside Reflex (Attacks & the Shell Clock).

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: Full player-facing definitions of the five stats — and which passive values (carry weight, HP, toxicity maximum) key off which stat — are not specified in the rebuild docs. The hooks above are the references that exist.
One Bonus Table
One table converts a stat into its bonus, everywhere, for every kind of roll. The left half is flesh; the right half is the chrome band.

content.label: MAX FLESH HP
content.text: Max Flesh HP = 16 + (2 × Grit bonus). Recalculate whenever Grit changes. Species, trait, and augment modifiers apply after.

There are no levels and no hit-point inflation: a fresh contractor holds 18–22 flesh HP and a trained-human ceiling sits at 24. Flesh is what you are; the shell you wear is the health bar that's supposed to take the hits — see Attacks & the Shell Clock.

Past 10, the roll bonus compresses — passives don't. Above 10 it takes two, then three, points of stat for each further +1 to the roll, while passive values that read the raw stat keep climbing point for point. Chrome makes you more machine faster than it makes you more skilled; the flesh gives less the further from human you push.
Training: 2 to 10
Stats train up to 10, paid in credits and downtime periods. The gate is never a rule — it's access, and access narrows as you climb.

rows: Nothing — a gym, a range, grinding reps. Anyone can get good.
An instructor or a professional facility. Past good needs coaching.
Specialist access: an academy, a faction, a master — and masters have agendas. Peak human is a relationship, not a purchase.

content.label: PATCHING A WEAKNESS
content.text: The bottom of the ladder is cheap: taking a stat from 4 to 6 costs 800 cr and four downtime periods. The top is where the calendar starts charging — downtime periods, not credits, are the scarce resource.
patch the weak stat first. it's never your best number that kills you.
How a maxed stat compares to the game's other flagship investments — tree mastery, a profession Name, a raised war-beast — lives on The Anchor Table.
The Chrome Band: 11–20
Training ends at 10. Every point past it is bought, not earned: chrome in the body, paid in credits and in flesh.

content.label: PER POINT ABOVE 10
items: −2 disruption, distributed to your other stats — you choose where it lands
Floor 1: no stat can be driven below 1

content.text: Disruption is part of the purchase, not a wound — the body is being restructured around the machine. Installation is splice work, and splice complications are Fate Roll territory (The Staircase).

The cap is a bill, not a rule. Reaching 20 means paying twenty points of disruption out of four other stats that each stop at a floor of 1. A fresh body does not have twenty points to give. The only road to 20 runs through years of training your whole array first, so the machine has something to consume — and what comes out the other side is a weapon with legs: peerless at one thing, crew-dependent for the rest.

content.label: DESIGN GAP
content.text: The chrome-band installation fee is stated as "plus installation" in the consolidation but never priced. The install ladder on Trees Overview prices augment tiers, not stat points.
Congo Industries Bodyworks thanks you for choosing to become more. Per-point pricing includes the point. Installation, disruption, and whoever you were are billed separately.
'become more.' more what is the part you sign away the right to ask.
